name: Messiah, the Anointed One
notes: The Hebrew title 'Messiah' (mashiach, H4899) and the word 'anointed' carry the very idea the Greek New Testament names 'Christ' (Christos, G5547) -- the Anointed One. The KJV keeps the words distinct in English ('Messiah'/'anointed' in the Old Testament, 'Christ' in the New), so the identity is easy to miss on the surface; John spells it out plainly. Psalm 2:2's 'his anointed' is taken up almost verbatim in Acts 4:26 ('his Christ'); Daniel 9:25-26's 'Messiah' is named 'Messias ... the Christ' in John 1:41 and 'Messias ... called Christ' in John 4:25. An AI-suggested starter weave -- review, adjust the kind, or extend it (e.g. 1 Samuel's 'the LORD's anointed', Isaiah 61:1 / Luke 4:18) as you see fit.

links.label: against the LORD, and against his anointed ... Christ
